zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough於1431年出生在英國林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛時，他的父親Thomas de Burgh26歲，母親Elizabeth Percy Lady of Atholl27歲。他於大約1460年與Margaret de Ros結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1471年登記服兵役。他去世於1496年3月18日在他的家鄉享年65歲，埋葬在英國林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough于1431年出生在英国林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛时，他的父亲Thomas de Burgh26岁，母亲Elizabeth Percy Lady of Atholl27岁。他于大约1460年与Margaret de Ros结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1471年登记服兵役。他去世于1496年3月18日在他的家乡享年65岁，埋葬在英国林可爾史爾蓋斯波洛。

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough was born in 1431, in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England, his father, Thomas de Burgh, was 26 and his mother, Elizabeth Percy Lady of Atholl, was 27. He married Margaret de Ros about 1460. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He registered for military service in 1471. He died on 18 March 1496, in his hometown, at the age of 65, and was buried in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough est né en 1431, à Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas de Burgh, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Percy Lady of Atholl, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Margaret de Ros environ 1460. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1471. Il est décédé le 18 mars 1496, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough 1431 in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas de Burgh 26 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Percy Lady of Atholl 27. Er hat um 1460 Margaret de Ros geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er hat sich 1471 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 18. März 1496 im Alter von 65 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough è nato il nel 1431 nel Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, Thomas de Burgh, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Percy Lady of Atholl, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Margaret de Ros circa 1460. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1471. Morì il 18 marzo 1496, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolto a Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsboroughが1431年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアガインスボロウで生まれた時，父Thomas de Burghは26歳，母Elizabeth Percy Lady of Athollは27歳であった。約1460年にMargaret de Rosと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1471年に兵役登録をした。彼は1496年3月18日に故郷で65歳で死去し，イギリスリンコルンシュアイアガインスボロウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough(이)가 1431년 영국 링코실르 게인스버루그h에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas de Burgh(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Percy Lady of Atholl(은)는 27세였다. 그는 대략 1460년 Margaret de Ros(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1471년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1496년 3월 18일 그의 고향에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 링코실르 게인스버루그h에 묻혔다.

mn.Wensleydale, Yorkshire, England, United: Wensleydale, Eoferwic, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough nasceu em 1431, em Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas de Burgh, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Percy Lady of Atholl, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret de Ros aproximadamente 1460. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1471. Ele faleceu em 18 de março de 1496, em sua cidade natal, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough родился в 1431 г. в местности геинсбороую, линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas de Burgh было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Percy Lady of Atholl, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret de Ros около 1460 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. В 1471 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 18 марта 1496 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности геинсбороую, линколншир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Thomas Burgh 1st Baron Burgh of Gainsborough nació en 1431, en Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas de Burgh, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Percy Lady of Atholl, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Margaret de Ros aproximadamente 1460. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1471. Murió el 18 de marzo de 1496, en su pueblo, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultado en Gainsborough, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.
